title: Kirst (pilot)
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Conflicting|"[[A Day in the Life]]"|''[[Heir to the Empire]]''}} {{FAreview}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Kirstnohelmet.jpg]] |name=Kirst |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{Tales|12|A Day in the Life}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]{{CSWECite|II|220|Kirst}} |pronouns=She/her{{CSWECite|II|220|Kirst}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blonde]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We're approaching some kind of… clearing. Filswik.''"
"''No. Not again.|Wister and Kirst, after coming across the remains of the ''Executor''|}} '''Kirst''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] who flew in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. She was close with her fellow Rogue, [[Wister]]. When the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] struck at the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] unfinished [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Kirst and the Rogues—temporarily rechristened [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] by [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]—flew their [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighters]] in the [[Space warfare/Legends|attack]]. The Empire had anticipated their strike, however, and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] saw the Alliance [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] fight off an onslaught from the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] until Antilles and several other [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were able to enter the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] superstructure and destroy the entire [[Space station/Legends|station]]. The Alliance remained at Endor for some [[Time/Legends|time]], and Rogue [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]] participated in post-battle cleanup operations. During one such operation, Kirst, Wister, and Antilles [[Starfighter combat/Legends|clashed with]] remaining Imperial starfighters and pursued a [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced x1]] into Endor's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] led them to a clearing that housed the remains of the downed [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], which [[Laser/Legends|fired]] its still-active [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and destroyed Wister's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. Kirst and Antilles then fired on a nearby canyon cliff, which created an avalanche that finished off the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|massive ship]], and returned to the Rebel [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']]. Kirst, severely shaken by Wister's [[Death/Legends|death]], was comforted by Antilles. ==Biography== === Service at Endor === Kirst was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]{{CSWECite|II|220|Kirst}} confirms that Kirst flew in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which is dated to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] in''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. as a member of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]],{{CSWECite|II|220|Kirst}} an elite group of twelve [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]].{{CSWECite|III|106|Rogue Squadron}} In that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], as the Alliance's [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] raged, the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] massed near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] in preparation for a major attack on the Empire's unfinished [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], which was [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Tree/Legends|forested]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].{{Film|VI}} [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] temporarily rechristened the Rogues [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] prior to the battle, in honor of a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] in which he had once served. Red Squadron was attached directly to the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the entire [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]].[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi'', Second Edition]] [[File:Xwing-cover-art.png|left|thumb|Kirst participated in the Battle of Endor.]] The fleet was not scheduled to arrive in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] until a [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] on Endor's surface disabled the [[SLD-26 planetary shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] that protected the Death Star, and before the battle, Red Squadron met in a briefing room, where Antilles told them that they would be entering the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] superstructure and firing on its [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]].{{WEGCite|story=The Briefing of Red Group|book=Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi}} Kirst and her squadron, flying [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], were with the fleet as it flew through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to Endor, and upon reaching their target, they discovered that the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] was still operational. Additionally, the Empire had anticipated their arrival, and the Rebels were forced to fend off attacking Imperial starfighters as they waited for the strike team to succeed. Although several Alliance squadrons were devastated by the [[Space warfare/Legends|onslaught]], Red Squadron [[Starfighter combat/Legends|scored]] a large number of [[Death/Legends|kills]] and remained mostly intact. The Death Star itself joined in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] when its [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] began taking out Rebel [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], and the Alliance Fleet consequently began to attack Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] at point-blank range in order to avoid the [[Space station/Legends|battlestation's]] powerful [[Laser/Legends|laser]]. Several of the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warships]] were destroyed, among them the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. When the strike team finally destroyed the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator]], Antilles and several other [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] entered the unfinished Death Star and knocked out the reactor core, which set off a chain reaction that ultimately destroyed the entire station. [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was one of the Imperials killed aboard the Death Star, but the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] fought on for nearly four more [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] before retreating,''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' and the Rebels celebrated their victory on the surface of the forest moon. The Alliance maintained a presence at Endor for some [[Time/Legends|time]],[[Star Wars (1977) 100|''Star Wars'' (1977) 100]] and Kirst flew with Rogue Squadron during post-battle cleanup operations. === Cleanup operation === {{Quote|I have a shot. Repeat, Three has a clean shot.''"
"''Negative, Three. That fighter's short-range. I want to see where it's headed.|Kirst and Wedge Antilles, while chasing a TIE Advanced x1|}}[[File:Adayinthecanyon.jpg|thumb|350px|Kirst, Wister, and Wedge Antilles pursue a TIE Advanced x1 through the canyons of Endor.]]Kirst had grown close with fellow Rogue [[Wister]] during their time together in the squadron, and during one cleanup operation, the two of them flew as Antilles's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmates]], respectively holding the [[Callsign/Legends|call signs]] of [[Rogue Three/Legends|Rogue Three]] and [[Rogue Two/Legends|Rogue Two]]. The three Rogues pursued several Imperial fighters that remained in the [[Star system/Legends|system]], and after Antilles destroyed a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]], the trio pursued a [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced x1]] toward Endor's surface. Although Kirst had a clean shot at their last enemy, Antilles told her to hold her fire, as he wanted to see where the fighter was going. After they entered the moon's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] led the trio through a series of rocky canyons and into a clearing, where the remains of the downed ''Executor'' laid. The [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|massive vessel's]] [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] were still operational, and their sudden fire destroyed Wister's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. Antilles told a stunned Kirst to hold together and transmitted attack coordinates to her fighter; the two then fired [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] at a canyon cliff, and the resulting avalanche finished off the ''Executor''. When their [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] detected no further hostiles, they returned to the ''Home One'', where a tearful Kirst hugged Antilles before leaving the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] with no words spoken. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|''WISTER!"
"''Keep it together, Three. Go evasive. Vector to three-oh-four.|Kirst and Antilles, after Wister's death|}} [[File:Kirst.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kirst in the cockpit]] Kirst was a member of Rogue Squadron, one of the Rebel Alliance's most elite starfighter squadrons. Rogue Leader Wedge Antilles felt that the Rogues who fought at the Battle of Endor had the same fire and determination as the original Red Squadron, and during the battle, they scored many kills. Kirst was horrified to come across the still-operational remains of the ''Executor'' after the battle and was devastated when its guns killed her wingmate, Wister. She held her emotions in check after Antilles prompted her to do so, but after landing aboard the ''Home One'', she let her tears flow and wrapped her squadron leader in a wordless hug. Kirst had [[Color/Legends|blonde]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and light skin. She wore a [[Rebel flight suit]] while in her X-wing. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Star Wars - Tales 12.jpg|left|thumb|Kirst appeared in *Star Wars Tales* 12.]] Kirst first appeared in "[[A Day in the Life]]," a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story that was included in [[Star Wars Tales 12|''Star Wars Tales'' 12]], an issue of [[Dark Horse Comics]]'s anthology-style ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' series and published on [[June 5]], [[2002]]. Written by [[Brett Matthews]], penciled by [[Adrian Sibar]], and colored by [[Guy Major]], the comic depicts Kirst as a member of Rogue Squadron in the days after the Battle of Endor. In [[2006]], Keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]] [[Leland Chee]] stated that every story from the first twenty issues of ''Tales'' that was not "completely outrageous or intentionally comic" should be considered ambiguously [[canon]]ical, with its status upgraded to fully canonical if it were referenced in a later, non-''Tales'' source.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM}} Both Kirst and her wingmate Wister received entries in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which cemented their places in ''[[Star Wars]]'' canon. The entries also added new continuity by establishing that both pilots actually served in Rogue Squadron during the Battle of Endor.{{CSWECite|III|332|Wister}} Although the squadron's Endor roster is briefed by Wedge Antilles in the [[1990]] short story "[[The Briefing of Red Group]]," this article endeavors to avoid the conjecture involved in definitively stating that Kirst appears in said story. The canon status of ''A Day in the Life'' presents several continuity issues. The story dates itself at three [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the Battle of Endor, but a different post-Endor timeline is given for Wedge Antilles and Rogue Squadron in the [[1993]] novel ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]''—the book has them leave Endor two days after the battle and spend three days in hyperspace before arriving at the planet [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]].''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' This article therefore treats the three-day date given in ''A Day in the Life'' as erroneous and assumes that the story takes place at some other point shortly after the battle. Another continuity error concerns the ''Executor'', the largely intact wreckage of which Kirst, Wister, and Antilles stumble across in ''A Day in the Life''. According to the [[1991]] novel ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', the ''Executor'' completely disintegrated after crashing into the Death Star during the Battle of Endor.''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' ==Appearances== *{{Tales|12|A Day in the Life}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Three/Legends|Rogue Three]] |years=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]{{Film|V}} |before-years=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |after=Eventually [[Nrin Vakil]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] |after-years=[[4 ABY/Legends|4.5 ABY]][[Nrin Vakil]] is flying with the [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogues]] as early as ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: The Warrior Princess]]'', which is dated to [[4 ABY/Legends|4.5 ABY]] by [[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 2|''Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume'' 2]].–[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]{{CSWECite|III|291|Vakil, Nrin}} states that [[Nrin Vakil]] resigns from [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] after the [[evacuation of Ciutric]], which is dated to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] by ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]''.}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Kirst (pilot)}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |fi=Kirst }} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
